columns.extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The introduction on page 1 further clarifies: 'In ARASENS, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to double-blind treatment with either darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' The Methods section on page 2 confirms: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 is Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel.
